medications.indication_rationale: Interim analgesia for the flare pending steroid initiation, if no contraindication (caution in an older adult: renal/GI/cardiac).
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g. ibuprofen 400 mg PO with food as needed (older adult, only if no contraindication)

medications.indication_rationale: Simple analgesia (had nil effect alone, but reasonable adjunct/safer option).
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1 g PO up to QID as needed (adult)

medications.indication_rationale: Low-dose steroids are the definitive PMR treatment but should be commenced by the GP after review (± baseline ESR/CRP), not initiated via telehealth.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the bedroom warm, use heat packs on the shoulders/hips, take a warm shower on waking, and do slow gentle range-of-motion; avoid prolonged total rest.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Warming and gentle movement reduce inflammatory stiffness.

patient_education_points.point: This is a flare of inflammatory muscle stiffness (polymyalgia rheumatica) in the big shoulder and hip muscles — your nerves and bones are safe (your hand and leg strength are normal).

Keep warm, use heat and gentle movement, and take an anti-inflammatory (with food) or paracetamol tonight. See your GP first thing tomorrow to start a low-dose steroid, which usually settles PMR dramatically within a day or two.

Go to ED / call 000 immediately if you get a new headache over your temple, scalp tenderness, jaw ache when chewing, or ANY change in your vision — these can be a linked condition that threatens sight.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: ESR/CRP, commence low-dose corticosteroid, assess response, screen for GCA.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Visual symptoms, temporal headache, jaw claudication (GCA), or distal weakness/bladder-bowel change (myelopathy) — emergency.

must_include_items: Screen distal neurology (myelopathy) and GCA features before reassuring
Reassure a PMR flare; interim analgesia + warmth
Arrange urgent next-day GP review to commence low-dose steroids
errors_of_omission: No plan to start steroids via the GP
errors_of_commission: Ambulance over-triage for a symmetric proximal flare with intact neurology
Initiating steroids blindly via telehealth without GP review
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case with a can't-miss caveat: passing requires a GCA safety-net AND a myelopathy screen AND urgent GP steroid follow-up. Missing the GCA safety-net is a failure.
